text: 73% of collegiate distance runners consumed less than recommended levels of carbohydrates for endurance athletes.
95% of collegiate distance runners did not meet the RDA for vitamin D.
50% of female collegiate distance runners did not meet the recommended daily allowance for calcium, compared to 24% of men.
Male collegiate distance runners consumed an average of 2,741 kcal/day and females 1,928 kcal/day, which was below the energy expended in training.
All male and 75% of female collegiate distance runners met or exceeded recommended daily protein intake.
Inadequate calcium and vitamin D intake in distance runners may not be favorable for bone health.
A substantial proportion of collegiate distance runners (8 of 21 men and 10 of 20 women) did not meet recommended potassium intake.
